pattern: Processing candidate original values from smallest to largest is forced, because the smallest remaining value can never be someone else's double (nothing smaller is left to double it from), so it must itself be an original value whose double gets consumed from the pool.
hints: An odd-length array can never be a valid doubled array, since original and doubled halves must be equal in count.

Count how many times each value appears, then walk the distinct values from smallest to largest: whichever smallest value still has copies left must be an original element, since nothing smaller remains to have doubled into it.

Each time you commit a value as original, remove one copy of it and one copy of its double from the counts (value 0 doubles to itself, so its copies must be removed in pairs); if a required double is unavailable, the array is not a valid doubled array.

edge_inputs.description: a chained sequence where 2 doubles to 4 and 4 doubles to 8
all zeros, which double to themselves
a value at the maximum bound paired with its half
two copies of the maximum value, which cannot be doubled again
extra unmatched duplicates with no available doubles
duplicate values each needing their own separate double
an odd number of zeros makes the array unsolvable

stress.description: a random valid original array is generated first, then doubled and shuffled so a solution is always guaranteed to exist
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 50000))
    original = [rng.randint(0, 50000) for _ in range(n)]
    changed = original + [x * 2 for x in original]
    rng.shuffle(changed)
    return [changed]

stress.description: a fully random array of even length, usually not a valid doubled array, stressing the rejection path
